items.text: Dana Whitfield; uses she/her or they/them pronouns (inferred from partner reference); lives in Portland
Product manager at a healthcare data company in Portland; currently mid-quarter with a roadmap review scheduled for Thursday 2025-02-06
Facing prioritization decision between interoperability work and analytics dashboard for roadmap review; needs to present a recommendation to exec team
Sam (partner of six years); described as 'great' support, especially during difficult times
Marcus (brother, lives in Denver); they are close but haven't seen each other in over a year, currently only phone contact; Dana wants to change this in 2025
Wants to see brother Marcus in person this year after over a year of phone-only contact

supersededBy.text: Plans to visit Marcus, Elena, and new niece Nina in Denver before summer 2025; Marcus offered 'the guest room is yours'; Dana described this as 'a thing I owe now, and I want to owe it'

items.text: Lost her mom recently (before 2025-02-03); described it as making this 'a heavy winter' and said it 'colors everything'; did not want to discuss details in this session
Priya (closest friend from grad school; ceramicist and freelance designer); they signed up for a Tuesday night pottery class together starting in February 2025
items.archivedReason: Both items describe the same relationship with Priya; merging captures the full arc from pottery class friendship through the loan conflict in one coherent entry

items.text: [as of 2025-02-12] Taking a Tuesday night pottery class with friend Priya; described as 'the first standing thing we've had on the calendar in years'

[2025-02-12] Mom had an old wooden sailboat, a 22-footer called the Dorothy Jean — named after Dana's grandmother. Mom taught Dana to sail on that boat. The boat has been sitting under a tarp in Priya's dad's barn out near Scappoose since mom got sick. On Sunday 2025-02-09, Dana went out, pulled the tarp off, and started restoration work (sanded one plank). Dana said: 'I've decided I'm going to restore her. Slowly. No deadline.' And: 'working with my hands on something of hers felt like the first right thing in months.'
[as of 2025-02-12] Restoring mom's sailboat (the Dorothy Jean) at a barn near Scappoose; working slowly with no deadline, described as therapeutic

Training for the Lakefront Marathon in October 2025; following a four-runs-per-week plan with long runs on Saturday mornings; completed 8 miles on Saturday 2025-02-22 along the river; described choosing this goal as needing 'something with a long arc, something that's just mine' this winter

supersededBy.text: Was training for Lakefront Marathon in October 2025; withdrew registration on Monday 2025-04-14 after ankle injury made completion impossible; described the training arc as 'the first future-shaped thing I'd built since mom'

items.text: Has been a casual runner for years; ran 5Ks and one half marathon about four years ago (circa 2021)
[2025-02-24] Speaking about signing up for the marathon, Dana said: 'It's the first time since mom that I've been excited about something in the future instead of dreading it.'
Elena: Elena (Marcus's partner); gave birth to daughter Nina on 2025-03-06
items.archivedReason: Elena and Nina can be described together as they're primarily significant through the birth event and family connection; reduces redundancy while preserving all emotional detail

items.text: Nina: Nina (niece, daughter of Marcus and Elena); born 2025-03-06; Dana described crying at her desk upon seeing the first photo of Nina in a knit hat
items.archivedReason: Elena and Nina can be described together as they're primarily significant through the birth event and family connection; reduces redundancy while preserving all emotional detail

items.text: [2025-03-09] Marcus called Dana from the hospital hallway at approximately 2am Denver time after Nina's birth just to say 'she's here.' Dana said: 'We haven't talked like that — voice shaking, no agenda — in years. Denver suddenly feels way too far away.'
Plans to visit Marcus, Elena, and new niece Nina in Denver before summer 2025; Marcus offered 'the guest room is yours'; Dana described this as 'a thing I owe now, and I want to owe it'
[as of 2025-03-09] Gathering tax documents for accountant; deadline 2025-03-20; missing one 1099 form
items.archivedReason: Tax deadline of 2025-03-20 has passed (current date is 2025-04-27); this task is completed or obsolete and no longer part of current state

items.text: [2025-03-14] Dana scattered Mom's ashes at Beacon Rock on March 14. The urn had been in her closet for months and she couldn't look at that side of the closet. She drove out to the Gorge alone on a clear, cold Friday morning before she could talk herself out of it. Beacon Rock was theirs — Mom used to take Dana there as a kid, and on the way up taught her to read cloud shapes: 'Not just "that one's a rabbit" — she had this whole taxonomy, what a mare's tail sky meant, when the anvil clouds meant get off the water.' Dana stood at the top, the sky doing 'exactly the kind of high thin herringbone she loved,' and let her go into the wind. On the way down Dana picked up a fir cone from the trail; it's on the windowsill above her kitchen sink now. She drove home, Sam had soup waiting, and she slept eleven hours. (2025-03-18)

[as of 2025-04-01] Sam received a job offer as Perception team lead at a robotics startup in Austin, Texas; described as 'genuinely his dream problem space' with a strong offer; decision deadline early May 2025; Dana and Sam are considering whether to relocate together

Facing decision about potential move to Austin for Sam's job offer; torn between supporting Sam's career opportunity and staying in Portland where 'this geography is load-bearing' for healing after mom's death; specifically concerned about leaving the Dorothy Jean restoration, Priya, pottery class, and the Gorge/Beacon Rock; Sam has said he won't take the offer 'if it costs us this year of me healing'; decision needed by early May 2025 (opened 2025-04-01)
[as of 2025-04-01] Marathon training at week six; completed 12 miles on Saturday (approximately 2025-03-29) with no complaints

items.text: [2025-04-01] Speaking about the potential move to Austin and leaving Portland, Dana said: 'Mom's here. Not literally anymore — but the Gorge, Beacon Rock, the barn. This geography is load-bearing for me right now.'

items.text: Priya: Approximately two weeks before 2025-04-27, Priya asked Dana to borrow eight thousand dollars for a ceramics studio lease deposit and buildout; Dana said no immediately and 'badly,' citing not mixing money and friendship; Priya went 'very quiet and then very polite'; they have skipped pottery class twice since; Dana described the exchange as delivering 'ten years of friendship a verdict in four seconds' and recognizes the delivery hurt more than the answer itself
items.archivedReason: Both items describe the same relationship with Priya; merging captures the full arc from pottery class friendship through the loan conflict in one coherent entry
